chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content:
Algorithmic Foundations of Big Data Computing. (3-0). Credit 3. Design and
analysis of algorithms to analyze large sets of data in fields such as machine
learning and data mining; streaming algorithms; parallel algorithms; sublinear time
algorithms; sketching algorithms; applications of these algorithms; theoretical
analysis of these algorithms as well as practical implementations for real problems.
Prerequisites: CSCE 629 or approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): CSCE 629 or approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• Describe the algorithmic challenges associated with datasets that are too large to fit into
the memory of a single computer.
• Describe and compare a variety of approaches that previously have been developed in the
research literature for this challenging setting.
• Develop and analyze their own algorithms for a variety of applications.

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Optional
No textbook needed

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Exams - Students' understanding of the material will be assessed using a sequence of three
proctored exams (see schedule below).
Graded Attendance – Class participation will be expected. Attendance will be taken at each
class. The grade for attendance will be the number of attended lectures, or excused absences
divided by the total number of classes. If a student has an excused absence, they should
provide documentation within one week after the missed class.
Project - Each student will do a project on a topic of their interest related to the course. The
project grade will be based on the implementation, and a presentation made to the class
presenting the project.

Grading policy:
• Attendance: 10%
• 3 exams covering material from class: 60% (20% each)
• Project: 20%
• Project presentation: 10%

Grade range:
• 90-100% A
• 80-90% B
• 70-80% C
• 60-70% D
• Below 60% F

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Plan for course each week
| Week | Activity |
|---|---|
| 1 | Introduction, background review |
| 2 | Background review |
| 3 | Sublinear time algorithms |
| 4 | Streaming algorithms |
| 5 | Streaming algorithms, Exam 1 |
| 6 | Coresets |
| 7 | Coresets |
| 8 | Data selection for ML |
| 9 | Data selection, Exam 2 |
| 10 | Parallel algorithms |
| 11 | Distributed algorithms |
| 12 | Applications |
| 13 | Applications, Exam 3 |
| 14 | Project presentations |
| 15 | Project presentations |
